channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Give GitHub Copilot CLI real code intelligence with language servers
item.description: Install and configure LSP servers for GitHub Copilot CLI, replacing brute-force grep/decompile with real code intelligence. The post Give GitHub Copilot CLI real code intelligence with language...

item.title: Architecting a Production-Ready Express + TypeScript Backend: Type Augmentation, Global Errors, and Middleware Factories
item.description: When building a personal finance tracker, data integrity and system reliability are non-negotiable. One missing try/catch block can crash your whole server, and weak types can let invalid...

item.title: You Don't Need Another Agent. You Need a Linter.
item.description: In my last post I complained — a lot — about product managers and how they made my life hell with vibe code. PS: apologies, manager, if you're reading this — but it's true. Now, I'm not here just...

item.title: Cybersecurity researchers aren’t happy about the guardrails on Anthropic’s Fable
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are complaining that Anthropic's new model Fable has guardrails that are too strict for any cybersecurity work.

item.title: G4 Fractional VMs are now available on Google Cloud!
item.description: In 2025 Google Cloud added G4, powered by NVIDIA's RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs to their offering, allowing them to offer hardware not only for AI applications, but also for other...

item.title: I built an open-source CLI that tells you if ChatGPT cites your brand — and what to do about it
item.description: Your users have started asking ChatGPT and Perplexity instead of Google. So here is the uncomfortable question: when someone asks an AI engine "what is the best tool for <your category>", does...

item.title: CLI over MCP: a small Chrome DevTools experiment in Copilot CLI
item.description: I ran the same browser smoke task through two paths: direct Chrome DevTools MCP and a custom CLI skill around mcp2cli. In GitHub Copilot CLI with gpt-5.3-codex-medium, direct Chrome DevTools MCP...

item.title: I Built My Own API Gateway in Rust — Here's What I Learned
item.description: Every backend project I've worked on eventually hits the same wall. You start clean — one service, simple routes, everything works. Then slowly the requirements creep in. "We need rate limiting."...

item.title: Proxy OpenAI Through Kong AI Gateway on Kubernetes
item.description: The Problem With Talking Directly to LLMs Most teams start by wiring their app straight to the OpenAI API. It works — until you need to add auth, rate limiting, observability, or swap out the...

item.title: Inngest + Next.js: The Complete Guide (2026)
item.description: Serverless functions have a time limit. Vercel gives you 60 seconds on Pro. None of that is enough to send a welcome email sequence, process an uploaded CSV, or run any workflow with multiple...

item.title: I am building a PDF API because every codebase I touch has a haunted PDF service
item.description: There is a file in almost every backend I have worked on. It generates PDFs. Invoices, mostly. Sometimes reports or certificates. It was written years ago by someone who left, it runs a headless...

item.title: Ivanti, Fortinet, and SAP Release Patches for Multiple Critical Vulnerabilities
item.description: Fortinet, Ivanti, and SAP have released security updates to address multiple critical security vulnerabilities that could result in arbitrary code execution and information disclosure. The...

item.title: Unpatched Langflow Flaw CVE-2026-5027 Exploited for Unauthenticated RCE
item.description: A high-severity unpatched security flaw in Langflow, an open-source low-code platform to build artificial intelligence (AI) applications, has come under active exploitation in the wild, according...

item.title: Datadog veterans launch AI coding startup Niteshift on a bet against Big AI lock-in
item.description: AI coding agent startup Niteshift has raised a $7 million seed round from a who's who of angels. It's betting companies will want power over, not lock-in with model makers.

item.title: Boox’s quirky page-turning remote won me over
item.description: Following the launch of the surprisingly popular Kobo Remote, Boox has released its own device to ease the burden of reaching for an e-reader’s touchscreen that’s an arm’s length away. The Tappy...

item.title: The Apple Watch Series 11 is back to its best price
item.description: Apple’s upcoming watchOS 27 update will bring Siri AI and other exciting features to the Apple Watch Series 9 and newer Apple Watch models. If you’re contemplating an upgrade, all colorways of the...

item.title: The three hard-tech moonshots fueling SpaceX’s unbelievable IPO
item.description: Most of the value in SpaceX's IPO is effectively a call option on the company's ambitious space data center plans.

item.title: CISA Adds Cisco, Chrome, and Arista Flaws to KEV Catalog Amid Active Exploitation
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added three new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, following reports of active...

item.title: Warner Music acquires AI attribution startup Sureel AI
item.description: Through the acquisition, WMG aims to better track when its artists' work is used in AI-generated content or for training AI models.

item.title: Valve is phasing out physical Steam gift cards due to scammers
item.description: After over a decade, Steam will no longer sell physical gift cards in stores. In a support page spotted earlier by Windows Central, Valve says it will no longer restock its gift cards once they...

item.title: Five questions for the duo behind The Pelley Minutes
item.description: Websites are so back! Today's website worth visiting is The Pelley Minutes, a clever project that puts the career of longtime 60 Minutes correspondent Scott Pelley into perspective. The show may...

item.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
item.description: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment...

item.title: The Social Reckoning trailer gives us our first look at Jeremy Strong as Zuck
item.description: Over 15 years after David Fincher and Aaron Sorkin's The Social Network premiered in theaters, Sony has released the first trailer for The Social Reckoning ahead of its theatrical release on...

item.title: The AirPods Pro 3 are $179 at Walmart, their best-ever price
item.description: Ahead of Prime Day, Walmart is offering the best price we’ve seen yet on the Apple AirPods 3 wireless earbuds. Originally $249 (though commonly selling somewhere between $200 and $220), they’re...

item.title: Wrongful Arrest Exposes Failures in One of the Oldest Police Face-Recognition Tools in the US
item.description: The ACLU is suing two Florida police departments over the arrest of a Fort Myers man in a child-abduction case, saying officers treated a flawed face-recognition match as a near-certain ID.

item.title: I’m relieved Siri AI isn’t trying to be a health coach
item.description: This is Optimizer, a weekly newsletter sent from Verge senior reviewer Victoria Song that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your life. This...

item.title: Google’s Nest Cam with Floodlight is selling at its lowest price yet
item.description: The Google Nest Cam with Floodlight is marked down to $179.99 ($100 off) at multiple retailers, including Amazon, Best Buy, Home Depot, and directly from Google. This weather-resistant outdoor...

item.title: AT&T is launching $3 ‘unlimited’ day passes for iPads
item.description: AT&T has introduced a new "Unlimited Day Pass" cellular data offer for iPad users who need brief connectivity instead of signing up for a long-term plan. The day pass is available for $3 in the US...

item.title: China Opens World’s First Wind-Powered Underwater Data Center
item.description: With an initial capacity of 24 megawatts, the innovative data center uses seawater as a natural cooling system.

item.title: Jedify raises $24M to help companies arm AI agents with context on their business
item.description: The funding round was led by Norwest, with participation S Capital VC, Cerca Partners, and Oceans Ventures. Snowflake Ventures also participated as a strategic investor.
